tasks.title: Create git-guard module: plugin/src/tools/git-guard.ts
tasks.summary: Fixed harden findings: guard now runs even if store unavailable, excludes primary checkout from worktree allowlist, logs degraded git fact-check paths, adds hook BLOCK integration test, adds parseWorktreePaths unit tests, and tightens docs.
tasks.implementation_summary: Fixed harden findings: guard now runs even if store unavailable, excludes primary checkout from worktree allowlist, logs degraded git fact-check paths, adds hook BLOCK integration test, adds parseWorktreePaths unit tests, and tightens docs.

tasks.title: Add hook-level integration test for bash interception: plugin/src/integration.test.ts
Wire git guard into tool.execute.before hook: plugin/src/index.ts

proposal.approval_evidence: Problem statement approved by user. Proposal updated to explicitly preserve valid same-project/different-branch OpenCode agent work through separate git worktrees and to keep OpenCode snapshot contention out of scope unless a fixable hook is found.

discovery.approval_evidence: Discovery complete. Codebase investigation mapped all git mutation paths: checkpoint.ts uses runGit (validated), archive helpers use execGit (validated), raw bash is the unguarded surface. tool.execute.before hook confirmed: input.tool === "bash" → output.args.command is inspectable/mutable, throw blocks execution. Design: layered guard (bash interception in hook + existing wrapper validation). 11 ACs confirmed, scope and success criteria updated from placeholder.

design.approval_evidence: Design validated with CAUTION verdict from adv-researcher. Refined v2 addresses: compound command splitting (shell operator parsing), git alias resolution with 5min TTL cache, -C/--git-dir workdir override parsing, fast-path short-circuit for non-mutation commands, new spec requirement rq-gm01 in advance-meta, documented residual risk (shell aliases/functions/scripts). Two-layer architecture confirmed sound: Layer 1 (bash hook interception) covers the only unguarded path; Layer 2 (existing checkpoint/archive validation) already validated.

planning.approval_evidence: 6-task graph approved by user. Tasks: (1) git-guard module, (2) unit tests, (3) hook wiring, (4) integration test, (5) spec delta, (6) docs. Execution order: 1→2, 1→3→4, 5+6 parallel. AC coverage complete.

execution.approval_evidence: All 6 tasks done. Files: git-guard.ts (new, ~495 lines), git-guard.test.ts (new, ~590 lines), index.ts (modified, +30 lines guard wiring), integration.test.ts (modified, +80 lines), advance-meta/spec.json (modified, +99 lines rq-gm01), ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md (modified, +25 lines). All checks pass: typecheck ✓, lint ✓, format ✓, 1942/1942 tests pass.

acceptance.approval_evidence: User accepted review/acceptance summary. Review verdict APPROVED after remediation. Verification: pnpm run check passed; pnpm test passed (1943 passed, 2 skipped).

release.approval_evidence: Archive sign-off received from user (`sign off`). Validation passed with warnings only. Conformance status: no locked specs configured. Harden verification: pnpm run check passed; pnpm test passed (1946 passed, 2 skipped); merge compatibility with trunk passed.

clarify_findings.message: Scope section is empty or placeholder. What specific files/modules will change?
Proposal describes behavior that could fail but doesn't mention error handling or rollback. What happens when things go wrong?
